content.text:
[cron:feee69f8-6016-4f8f-a09c-08b39d9a9191 github-backup] Run these commands: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace && git add -A && git diff --cached --quiet && echo "nothing to commit" || (git commit -m "auto backup" && git push). Report result to Nemesis via message(action=send, channel=telegram, target=7924461837) only if there was an error. If nothing changed, reply NO_REPLY.
Current time: Sunday, April 12th, 2026 - 4:01 PM (UTC) / 2026-04-12 16:01 UTC

content.thinking:
This is a cron job that runs git auto-backup. I need to execute the commands and only report to Nemesis if there was an error.

The cron job asks me to:
1. cd /root/.openclaw/workspace && git add -A && git diff --cached --quiet && echo "nothing to commit" || (git commit -m "auto backup" && git push)

This will:
- Add all changes to staging
- Check if there are staged changes (--cached --quiet returns 0 if no changes, 1 if there are changes)
- If nothing to commit, echo "nothing to commit"
- If there are changes, commit and push

I should run this silently and only message if there's an error.

arguments.command: cd /root/.openclaw/workspace && git add -A && git diff --cached --quiet && echo "nothing to commit" || (git commit -m "auto backup" && git push)

content.thinking: Done - reported the error to Nemesis as instructed.

content.text: Reported the error to Nemesis. The push was blocked by GitHub's secret scanning protection - it found what looks like a GitHub Personal Access Token in the session files. He'll need to unblock it at the provided URL.
